commit.message:
chore: bump version to 0.2.0 (#16)

* feat: bump @noble/curves+hashes to v2, eslint to 10, jest to 30 (#15)

Combined dependency upgrade addressing dependabot PRs #9-#14:

- @noble/curves ^1.8→^2.0.1, @noble/hashes ^1.7→^2.0.1
  - Migrate import paths to .js extensions and new module locations
  - Update secp256k1 API: randomSecretKey, Uint8Array inputs, prehash:false
  - Simplify ecdsaVerify (format:'der' option replaces manual normalization)
- eslint ^8→^10, @typescript-eslint ^7→^8
  - Migrate .eslintrc.json to flat config (eslint.config.mjs)
- jest ^29→^30, @types/jest ^29→^30
  - Add transformIgnorePatterns for ESM-only noble packages
  - Fix --testPathPattern→--testPathPatterns in CI
- Drop Node 18 from CI matrix (noble v2 requires >=20.19)
- Update moduleResolution to "bundler" for .js extension support

All 279 tests pass across 11 suites.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: bump version to 0.2.0

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct schema types in associations OpenAPI spec

Fields like firstObjectLabel, firstObjectKey, secondObjectLabel,
secondObjectKey, and associationType were typed as "object" but
had string examples — causing Mintlify OpenAPI validation to fail.
Changed all to type "string" to match their actual values.

commit.message:
Prevent Civitai staff impersonation (#2059)

* Prevent Civitai staff impersonation across comments and usernames

Expand auto-mute from Chat-only to Comment/CommentV2 entities, add
"civit" and leet-speak variants to the username partial blocklist,
and enforce isUsernamePermitted server-side during onboarding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add unit tests for isUsernamePermitted blocklist logic

Tests exact blocklist matching, partial "civit" substring blocking,
leet-speak variants, and allowed normal usernames against the JSON
blocklist directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add dynamic username blocklist management for moderators

Extend BlocklistType with UsernameExact/UsernamePartial so mods can
manage blocked usernames via the existing /moderator/blocklists UI
without code deploys. Makes isUsernamePermitted async to check both
the static JSON baseline and the dynamic DB/Redis/in-memory cache.

Fix minigames tab not showing - add tab navigation support

Fixes:
- Added minigamesTab to hidden tabs list in showTab() function
- Added minigames tab case to showTab() with proper button activation
- Calls setupMinigameTab() when minigames tab is selected
- Fixed tab button indexing for 4th tab (index [3])
- Now minigames tab properly shows content and active state

The 🎮 Ігри tab now works correctly with maze game! 🎯

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: update memory files — Mar 4 evening, reliability hardening + DAY TIF fix

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: deploy function to iad1 (us-east-1) near Neon DB

Function was deploying to bom1 (Mumbai) while Neon DB is in us-east-1.
Each DB query had ~300ms cross-region latency, causing registration to
exceed the 10s timeout (bcrypt + Neon cold start + 4-5 DB roundtrips).

Pinning to iad1 puts the function in the same region as the DB,
reducing per-query latency from ~300ms to ~1-5ms.

Add auth gate, invite-aware auth screens, and profile sign-out

Prompt unauthenticated users with a login modal on first open while
preserving guest browsing via a hasSeenAuthPrompt flag. When an invite
deep link is present, eagerly fetch invite details (entity name, role,
sender, placeholder person) and display a rich context card on both
login and register screens. Registration pre-fills name/email from
placeholder person data. Post-auth navigation skips router.dismiss
when a pending invite exists, letting the auth listener route to the
acceptance screen. Also adds a sign-out button to the profile screen
and shows the user's first initial in the profile avatar.

commit.message:
fix(db): sanitize credentials from database error messages

Connect() and ConnectAdmin() now strip passwords from error messages
before returning, preventing credential leakage in CLI output and logs.
Changed error wrapping from %w to %s to break the DSN exposure chain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 4 employee app bugs: tutorial popup, GH sowing crash, overlay blocking, GPS speed

1. Tutorial auto-start DISABLED — no more popup on login. Manual ? button still works.
2. GH sowing render crash: forEach(function(t)) shadowed the t() i18n function,
   causing TypeError when building button labels. Renamed to forEach(function(task)).
3. Tutorial overlay/bubble CSS: opacity:0 → display:none (same fix pattern as
   seed prompt overlay — prevents invisible elements from blocking touch events).
4. GPS capture: enableHighAccuracy:false, timeout 3s (was 10s). Coarse location
   is sufficient for farm clock-in, dramatically reduces wait time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add @netlify/plugin-nextjs dependency for SSR support

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add early stopping to inverse parameter optimization

Inverse optimization (Adam/L-BFGS) was running all 1200 steps even when
loss plateaued after ~50 steps, wasting compute. Added --early-stop-patience
(default 100) and --early-stop-tol (default 1e-6) to break out early.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix no-set-time showcase box collapse; add Tweedy's cluster — fw-v129

Showcase box was collapsing to ~1 row because start=end="12:00" when
all shows share the same start time and have no end times. Fix: exclude
no_set_time shows from renderShowcaseOverlays entirely; instead show the
showcase name in the no-set-time cell header (single showcase → name;
mixed showcases → "(No Set Times)" as before).

Rowspan now also scales by number of shows (length*2 slots minimum) so
cells with many pills (e.g. 7 artists at Tweedy's) have room to expand.

venues.json: add ["Tweedy's", "29th Street Ballroom"] cluster.

commit.message:
test: add unit tests for conflict-detector and config-loader

conflict-detector (17 tests):
- detectScheduleConflicts: no conflicts (no schedule configured), unavailable day
  (has schedule but not for this day), isAvailable=0 record, no conflict when
  marked available, blackout period → error with reason, double_booking when
  pilot in personsAssigned, no double_booking when pilot absent, weekly job limit
  warning (warning does not block scheduling), canSchedule=false on errors,
  canSchedule=true on warnings only
- isAvailable: returns true with no blackouts, returns false with overlapping blackout
- getPilotAssignments: empty range, pilot in assignments, pilot not in assignments,
  invalid JSON skipped, multiple assignments filtered correctly

config-loader (16 tests):
- getConfig: cache hit (no DB call), DB fetch on miss, caches after fetch,
  global values applied, app-specific overrides global, non-overridden globals
  preserved, JSON object parsing, JSON array parsing, plain string kept,
  empty result
- getConfigValue: existing key, missing key → null, JSON parsed value
- invalidateConfig: specific app key deleted, all 4 keys deleted without arg,
  admin-only deletion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add active consultation triggers to SOUL.md and Ann's instructions

Experts were handling multi-domain questions solo despite having
consultation tools available. Added:

- Shared SOUL.md: PRE-CONDITION Collaboration check — domain-specific
  triggers for when to consult each expert
- Ann's SOUL.md: Specific pre-response checklist — research keywords
  trigger Leslie consultation, complex SQL triggers Ben, etc.

Reinforces that solo answers to cross-domain questions are worse
than consulting specialists. 60s sync timeout makes it fast.

README.md rewritten as generic template README (was entirely Don-specific).
45 additional files parameterized for brand-specific references:
- Framework names (Pilot to Production, Data Readiness, Architect-Executor)
- Talk venues (TEDx, SaaS North, Plug & Play, McGill)
- City references (Montreal)
- Audience metrics (follower counts, subscriber counts)
- UI logos and avatar image

commit.message:
feat(ci): skip executor Docker build when build inputs unchanged (#161)

Computes a content hash (sha256sum) of executor/ and pkg/ (the only
directories COPYed into the Dockerfile), then checks Artifact Registry
for an existing content-$HASH tag. On cache hit, retags with the commit
SHA and latest via `gcloud artifacts docker tags add` (no pull needed).
On cache miss, performs the full build and pushes all three tags.

Saves ~2m on the deploy pipeline critical path for most merges (~43/45
monthly) where executor code hasn't changed.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Difix3D+ novel data reuse (Phase 2) to MILo integration

Implements the second half of the Difix3D+ baseline:

- Novel data pool: after each fix cycle, (synth_cam, enhanced_img_cpu)
  pairs are stored (cap: 200 entries, ~2.3 GB CPU RAM).
- Phase 2 reuse: at every training iteration after the first fix cycle,
  with probability `difix3d_lambda` (default 0.3), a random pool entry
  is moved to GPU and used for one extra gradient step — mirroring the
  `novel_data_lambda` mechanism in simple_trainer_difix3d.py.
- difix3d_views default bumped 16 → 48 (≈ 1/8 of a 450-camera dataset,
  matching Difix3D+ baseline).
- New CLI flags: --difix3d_lambda (train_wrapper + train.py patch).
- Dockerfile CACHE_BUST bumped to 3 to force patch re-application.

fix: detectar human takeover quando Agent Bot não envia webhook de msg humana

O Agent Bot do Chatwoot NÃO envia webhook message_created para mensagens
outgoing de agentes humanos (webhook_sendable? retorna false). Quando um
humano responde diretamente no Chatwoot, human_takeover nunca era setado
e a IA respondia por cima do humano.

Fix: consumer verifica via API do Chatwoot se há mensagens outgoing recentes
de agentes humanos quando a conversa está "open". Poller também verifica
nas mensagens embarcadas. Em ambos os casos, seta human_takeover e bloqueia IA.

commit.message:
refactor(call): extract VideoCall.jsx into focused custom hooks (#1200)

* refactor(call): extract VideoCall.jsx into focused custom hooks

Decompose the 1622-line VideoCall.jsx monolith into 11 focused hooks,
reducing it to a ~290-line orchestrator. No behavior changes — all 459
component tests pass across chromium, firefox, and webkit.

New hooks:
- useDisplayNameSync: mirror Empirica name → Daily displayName
- useDailyIdTracking: store dailyId/dailyIdHistory on player
- useCallStartSignaling: signal server to start recording
- useVisibilityTracking: log page blur/focus for debugging
- useTrackMonitor: poll for ended tracks, auto-recover
- usePermissionMonitor: watch Permissions API for revocations
- useCallLifecycle: join/leave Daily room + Firefox stall detection
- useDeviceErrors: device errors + fatal + network with priority dedup
- useGesturePrompt: gesture-gated operations + computed prompt state
- useDeviceRecovery: devicechange auto-recovery (W4)
- useDeviceAlignment: consolidated 3x ~120-line alignment functions

Part of #1199

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(call): address Copilot review feedback on extracted hooks

- Fix functional updater bug: useDeviceErrors setters don't support
  React-style functional updaters, so replace setError(prev => ...)
  with conditional setError(null) using the actual error values
- Add explicit type guard in useDeviceRecovery to only handle
  camera-error/mic-error/speaker-error (avoid mis-routing)
- Track and clear inline setTimeout IDs in useCallLifecycle cleanup
  to prevent state updates after unmount
- Add isDestroyed guard inside useTrackMonitor polling interval
- Replace high-cardinality navigator.userAgent Sentry tag with
  coarse browser name (Chrome/Safari/Firefox/Edge/Other)
- Fix misleading AGC success log (the call is currently a no-op)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
